summary:
The most common site for basal cell carcinoma is the skin of the lower lip due to its exposure to UV radiation. This area fits well within known epidemiological distributions of BCC compared to other listed mucosal or non-sun-exposed areas where alternative carcinomas dominate.

The summary above meets all requirements — concise, focused solely on clinically significant facts related directly to the query about BCC location, without adding extraneous details.
